trackers.name: Office of the Colorado State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender representing anyone accused of a crime who cannot afford counsel — 21 regional trial offices across 23 judicial districts, plus an Appellate Division in Denver.

trackers.name: Colorado Office of the Alternate Defense Counsel
trackers.desc: Provides court-appointed counsel in Colorado cases where the State Public Defender has a conflict of interest.

trackers.name: Connecticut Division of Public Defender Services
trackers.desc: Statewide indigent defense agency, including a Capital Defense and Trial Services Unit and the Connecticut Innocence Project / Post-Conviction Unit.

trackers.name: Delaware Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender offices providing criminal defense to people who cannot afford an attorney.

trackers.name: Iowa State Public Defender
trackers.desc: State agency providing legal representation to indigent people charged with crimes in Iowa.

trackers.name: Kansas State Board of Indigents’ Defense Services
trackers.desc: Oversees and provides indigent criminal defense across Kansas.

trackers.name: Kentucky Department of Public Advocacy
trackers.desc: Kentucky’s statewide public defender agency — also houses the Kentucky Innocence Project and a Capital Post-Conviction Unit.

trackers.name: Louisiana Public Defender Board
trackers.desc: Oversees Louisiana’s district public defender offices and statewide indigent defense delivery.

trackers.name: Mississippi Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender office serving people charged with crimes who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Mississippi Office of Capital Post-Conviction Counsel
trackers.desc: Represents people sentenced to death in Mississippi during post-conviction proceedings.

trackers.name: Missouri Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Missouri’s statewide public defender system providing trial, appellate and capital representation.

trackers.name: West Virginia Public Defender Services
trackers.desc: State agency administering public defender corporations and appointed counsel across West Virginia.

trackers.name: Wisconsin State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender with offices across Wisconsin, providing trial and appellate representation.

trackers.name: Neighborhood Justice Center
trackers.desc: Saint Paul nonprofit providing holistic criminal defense and support to low-income clients.

trackers.name: Legal Rights Center
trackers.desc: Minneapolis community-based public defense and restorative-justice organisation.

trackers.name: Minnesota Board of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Oversees Minnesota’s statewide and district public defender offices.

trackers.name: Public Defender Service for the District of Columbia
trackers.desc: Independent federally funded defender organisation representing indigent people in DC criminal and mental-health cases.

trackers.name: Montana Office of the State Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender with four divisions and over 20 offices — represents not only people accused of crimes but parents and children in child-welfare cases, elderly people losing autonomy, and people facing civil commitment.

trackers.name: Montana Innocence Project
trackers.desc: Investigates and litigates claims of actual innocence in Montana.

trackers.name: Committee for Public Counsel Services (CPCS)
trackers.desc: Massachusetts’ statewide public defender agency, providing trial and appellate representation through staff attorneys and assigned counsel.

trackers.name: Washington State Office of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Administers and improves indigent defense across Washington State’s trial and appellate courts.

trackers.name: Vermont Office of the Defender General
trackers.desc: Statewide public defense and assigned counsel for Vermonters who cannot afford an attorney.

trackers.name: Virginia Indigent Defense Commission
trackers.desc: Operates Virginia’s public defender offices and sets standards for court-appointed counsel.

trackers.name: Rhode Island Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender providing criminal, juvenile and appellate representation.

trackers.name: South Carolina Commission on Indigent Defense
trackers.desc: Administers indigent criminal defense across South Carolina’s circuits.

trackers.name: Justice 360
trackers.desc: South Carolina nonprofit representing people facing the death penalty, serving long sentences, or sentenced as children.

trackers.name: Maryland Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Statewide public defender providing representation in criminal, juvenile and appellate matters.

trackers.name: Michigan State Appellate Defender Office (SADO)
trackers.desc: Provides appellate defense to indigent Michigan defendants and maintains a national criminal defense resource library.

trackers.name: Federal Public Defender — District of Nebraska
trackers.desc: Federal defender office representing people charged with federal crimes in Nebraska who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Federal Public Defender — District of Nevada
trackers.desc: Federal defender office for the District of Nevada.

trackers.name: Rocky Mountain Innocence Center
trackers.desc: Investigates wrongful convictions in Nevada, Utah and Wyoming.

trackers.name: Georgia Innocence Project
trackers.desc: Nonprofit representing Georgia’s wrongfully convicted — has freed and exonerated 16 people, reviews hundreds of assistance requests each year, and litigates dozens of active innocence cases.

trackers.name: Los Angeles County Public Defender
trackers.desc: The first public defender agency in the United States (est. 1913) and the largest — represents adults, juveniles and people in mental-health proceedings in LA County who cannot afford private counsel. Runs record-clearing clinics across the county.

trackers.name: LA County Alternate Public Defender
trackers.desc: Handles Los Angeles County cases where the Public Defender has a conflict of interest — full criminal defense for indigent clients.

trackers.name: Harris County Public Defender’s Office
trackers.desc: Houston-area public defender (est. 2011) with a client-centered, holistic model and enforced caseload limits — lawyers, investigators and social workers working together; 37% of felony clients leave with no conviction.

trackers.name: Law Office of the Cook County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Chicago’s public defender — one of the largest criminal-defense firms in the US with 450+ attorneys, representing tens of thousands of Cook County residents; runs Freedom Defense Centers connecting clients to community services.

trackers.name: King County Department of Public Defense
trackers.desc: Seattle-area public defense for adults and youth who cannot afford an attorney — 24/7 on-call attorney line, financial screening without appointment, and a Blake Refund Bureau to vacate drug-possession records.

trackers.name: Orleans Public Defenders
trackers.desc: New Orleans public defender representing ~85% of people charged in Orleans Parish (19,487 cases in 2025) — client-centered model with social workers, client advocates and mitigation planning, rebuilt after Hurricane Katrina into a national benchmark.

trackers.name: The Bronx Defenders
trackers.desc: Holistic public defender in the South Bronx (est. 1997) — criminal, civil, family and immigration lawyers plus social workers and advocates address the full fallout of a criminal charge. Winner of the NLADA Clara Shortridge Foltz Award.

trackers.name: Brooklyn Defender Services
trackers.desc: Public defense for Brooklyn (and, since July 2025, Queens criminal cases) — free criminal, family, immigration and civil defense, with advocacy on housing, benefits, education and employment.

trackers.name: New York County Defender Services (NYCDS)
trackers.desc: Manhattan’s public defender since 1997 — 70+ trial attorneys plus social workers and investigators defending people in Manhattan Criminal and Supreme Courts at no cost, with social-service support built in.

trackers.name: Neighborhood Defender Service of Harlem
trackers.desc: Community-based, holistic public defense in Northern Manhattan — an early pioneer of the team-defense model integrating criminal, civil and family representation with community advocacy.

trackers.name: San Francisco Public Defender
trackers.desc: City-and-county public defender since 1921 — defends indigent people charged under California law, runs a Clean Slate record-clearing program and an immigration unit, and has exposed police misconduct leading to dropped cases.

trackers.name: Defender Association of Philadelphia
trackers.desc: Philadelphia’s public defender since 1934 — a nonprofit law firm with an integrated team of lawyers, social workers, mitigation specialists and investigators representing indigent adults and juveniles in criminal, delinquency and dependency cases, plus reentry and record-clearing programs.

trackers.name: Alameda County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Oakland-area public defender — primary defense counsel for indigent people facing incarceration in Alameda County, with client-centered representation, wrap-around services and a Clean Slate record-clearing program. Led by Brendon D. Woods.

trackers.name: Miami-Dade Public Defender (11th Judicial Circuit)
trackers.desc: Public defender for Florida’s 11th Judicial Circuit, handling ~75,000 cases a year — represents indigent people facing loss of liberty, with community-education programs and reentry help to seal/expunge records and restore civil rights.

trackers.name: Maricopa County Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Phoenix-area public defender — effective legal representation for indigent people facing criminal charges and mental-health commitments in Maricopa County Superior and Justice Courts.

trackers.name: Travis County Public Defender’s Office
trackers.desc: Austin-area public defender providing indigent criminal defense in Travis County, Texas.

trackers.name: San Diego County Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: San Diego County’s indigent defense — four ethically-walled offices (Primary, Alternate, Multiple Conflicts, Assigned Counsel) with mental-health and juvenile units; filed 5,000+ Prop 47 petitions to reduce felony convictions.

trackers.name: Hennepin County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Minneapolis / Fourth Judicial District public defender — represents juveniles and adults in misdemeanor, gross-misdemeanor and felony cases from arraignment through trial, sentencing and probation violations. Funded by both Minnesota and Hennepin County.

trackers.name: Allegheny County Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Pittsburgh / Allegheny County public defender — provides legal counsel in any proceeding where representation is constitutionally required for people who cannot afford a lawyer.

trackers.name: Office of the Public Defender, Atlanta Judicial Circuit
trackers.desc: Public defender for Fulton County / Atlanta (est. 1967) with 200+ staff across three locations — divisions for trials, appeals, juvenile, probation revocation and misdemeanors, representing indigent people from initial appearance through appeal.

trackers.name: City of Atlanta Public Defender
trackers.desc: The City of Atlanta’s municipal public defender — represents people charged in Atlanta Municipal Court who cannot afford counsel, a city-level office separate from the Fulton County / circuit defender.

trackers.name: Sacramento County Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Serving Sacramento County since 1948 — free defense in adult criminal, juvenile-delinquency and mental-health conservatorship cases, with a whole-person approach across seven divisions.

trackers.name: Riverside County Office of the Public Defender
trackers.desc: Riverside County public defender — court-appointed defense for people who cannot afford a private attorney in California state courts.

trackers.name: Santa Clara County Public Defender
trackers.desc: San Jose / Silicon Valley public defender — indigent criminal defense across Santa Clara County with multiple office locations.

trackers.name: Contra Costa County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Contra Costa County (East Bay) public defender — court-appointed criminal defense for indigent residents.

trackers.name: San Bernardino County Public Defender
trackers.desc: San Bernardino County public defender — indigent criminal defense across California’s largest county by area, with multiple locations.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Bureau of Buffalo
trackers.desc: Erie County / Buffalo’s criminal-defense and civil legal-aid provider (est. ~1902) — represents indigent people charged with violations, misdemeanors and felonies in Buffalo, handles indigent criminal appeals across Western NY, and runs a reentry unit for record-sealing and civil-disability relief.

trackers.name: Dallas County Public Defender’s Office
trackers.desc: Dallas County public defender — handles everything from misdemeanors to murder trials, with criminal, mental-health, appellate, DNA and capital divisions, plus juvenile, CPS and family units; represents people the courts have found indigent.

trackers.name: Bexar County Public Defender’s Office
trackers.desc: San Antonio / Bexar County public defender — client-centered, trauma-informed representation with divisions for misdemeanors, felonies, mental-health cases, magistration/bail hearings, appeals and post-conviction relief.

trackers.name: Metropolitan Public Defender (Portland)
trackers.desc: The largest trial-level public defender in Oregon (est. 1971, nonprofit) — serves Multnomah and Washington counties across criminal, juvenile, mental-health and specialty courts, plus a Community Law Division for record expungement, felony reduction and civil legal services.

trackers.name: Multnomah Defenders, Inc.
trackers.desc: Portland nonprofit public-defense firm — criminal, juvenile and appellate representation for indigent people in Multnomah County.

trackers.name: Pierce County Department of Assigned Counsel
trackers.desc: Tacoma / Pierce County’s public-defense department (Department of Assigned Counsel) — provides indigent criminal and juvenile defense and also serves the municipal courts of Tacoma, Gig Harbor and Fircrest. (Listed in the Washington State Office of Public Defense directory.)

trackers.name: Snohomish County Public Defender Association
trackers.desc: Everett-area nonprofit public defender — indigent criminal defense for Snohomish County residents.

trackers.name: Spokane County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Spokane County’s public-defender office — indigent criminal defense in eastern Washington. (Listed in the Washington State Office of Public Defense directory.)

trackers.name: Mecklenburg Defenders
trackers.desc: Charlotte / Mecklenburg County public defender — top-quality criminal representation for people who cannot afford private counsel, part of North Carolina’s statewide defender system.

trackers.name: Clark County Public Defender
trackers.desc: The largest criminal-defense firm in Nevada, serving Las Vegas and Clark County — specialized teams for juvenile, homicide, sexual-assault and appellate work; qualified for death-penalty defense under Nevada Supreme Court Rule 250.

trackers.name: Louisville–Jefferson County Public Defenders (KY DPA)
trackers.desc: The Kentucky Department of Public Advocacy’s Louisville Trial Office — serves indigent clients in Jefferson County. (Assumed these services on 1 July 2024 when the former Louisville-Metro Public Defender Corporation was dissolved under HB 568.)

trackers.name: Monroe County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Rochester / Monroe County public defender — client-centered defense in criminal, family and appellate matters, with attorneys, social workers and investigators working as a team.

trackers.name: Monroe County Conflict Defender
trackers.desc: Created in 2003 to represent Monroe County clients where the Public Defender has a conflict of interest — criminal and family court cases in Rochester City Court, Family Court and state appellate courts.

trackers.name: Wayne County Indigent Defense Services (IDSD)
trackers.desc: Detroit / Wayne County’s indigent-defense department (launched 2020) — manages the county public-defender and assigned-counsel systems for felony criminal defense.

trackers.name: Neighborhood Defender Service of Detroit
trackers.desc: Holistic felony defense for Wayne County residents — attorneys, staff investigators and social workers working as a team so no one is convicted because they are poor; part of the national NDS model.

trackers.name: Franklin County Public Defender (Columbus)
trackers.desc: Columbus / Franklin County public defender — comprehensive representation in criminal, juvenile and custody proceedings for indigent people, fulfilling the constitutional mandate of equal justice.

trackers.name: Hamilton County Public Defender (Cincinnati)
trackers.desc: Cincinnati / Hamilton County public defender — zealous, ethical defense protecting the statutory and constitutional rights of indigent clients.

trackers.name: Butler County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Butler County, Ohio public defender (Hamilton, OH) — indigent criminal defense for county residents.

trackers.name: Marion County Public Defender Agency (Indianapolis)
trackers.desc: Indianapolis / Marion County public defender — an independent agency representing indigent people in felony, misdemeanor, problem-solving and family courts and on appeal, with paralegal, investigator and social-worker support teams.

trackers.name: Public Defender of Metropolitan Nashville & Davidson County
trackers.desc: Metropolitan public defender for Nashville and Davidson County.

trackers.name: Law Office of the Shelby County Public Defender (Memphis)
trackers.desc: Memphis / Shelby County public defender — established 1917 as the first public defender east of the Mississippi; high-quality, client-centered defense for indigent people, a longtime regional and national leader in equal justice.

trackers.name: Cuyahoga County Public Defender (Cleveland)
trackers.desc: Cleveland / Cuyahoga County public defender — felony, misdemeanor (Cleveland & Lakewood municipal courts), juvenile, appellate and child-welfare defense, plus record sealing/expungement and post-conviction relief.

trackers.name: Missouri State Public Defender — St. Louis City District
trackers.desc: The Missouri State Public Defender’s St. Louis City trial office — indigent criminal defense for the City of St. Louis (part of Missouri’s statewide district-based defender system).

trackers.name: Missouri State Public Defender — Kansas City (Jackson County)
trackers.desc: The Missouri State Public Defender’s Kansas City trial office — indigent criminal defense for Jackson County (part of Missouri’s statewide district-based defender system).

trackers.name: Pima County Public Defender (Tucson)
trackers.desc: Tucson / Pima County public defender (est. 1970) — felony, juvenile-delinquency, dependency and appellate defense with investigators, social workers and mitigation specialists; handles cases up to capital homicide.

trackers.name: Salt Lake Legal Defender Association
trackers.desc: Salt Lake County’s nonprofit public defender (est. 1965) — holistic, client-centered representation in all felony, misdemeanor and specialized juvenile cases; 198+ staff funded by Salt Lake County and City.

trackers.name: City of Tucson Public Defender
trackers.desc: The City of Tucson’s municipal public defender — represents indigent people facing criminal prosecution in Tucson City Court, distinct from the Pima County defender.

trackers.name: Colorado State Public Defender — Denver Trial Office
trackers.desc: The Denver trial office of the Colorado State Public Defender — part of an independent statewide system (est. 1970) of 21 regional offices serving all 64 Colorado counties; client-centered defense for people who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Law Office of the DeKalb County Public Defender
trackers.desc: DeKalb County / Stone Mountain Judicial Circuit public defender (metro Atlanta) — 100+ staff providing zealous defense in all criminal cases, including youth prosecuted as adults and death-penalty cases.

trackers.name: Public Defender, 17th Judicial Circuit (Broward)
trackers.desc: Fort Lauderdale / Broward County public defender (Florida’s 17th Judicial Circuit) — defends indigent people in cases from misdemeanors to capital crimes, with evidence-based rehabilitation programs to reduce recidivism.

trackers.name: Public Defender, 9th Judicial Circuit (Orange–Osceola)
trackers.desc: Orlando-area public defender (Florida’s 9th Judicial Circuit, serving Orange and Osceola counties) — indigent criminal defense including a dedicated Children’s Defense Division.

trackers.name: Public Defender, 13th Judicial Circuit (Hillsborough)
trackers.desc: Tampa / Hillsborough County public defender (Florida’s 13th Judicial Circuit) — the largest criminal-defense firm in the county with 220+ staff and 120+ attorneys; holistic model addressing homelessness, mental health and addiction alongside the case.

trackers.name: Public Defender, 15th Judicial Circuit (Palm Beach)
trackers.desc: Palm Beach County public defender (Florida’s 15th Judicial Circuit) — indigent criminal defense for people who cannot afford private counsel.

trackers.name: Public Defender, 4th Judicial Circuit (Duval)
trackers.desc: Jacksonville / Duval County public defender (Florida’s 4th Judicial Circuit, also serving Clay and Nassau counties) — indigent criminal defense across northeast Florida.

trackers.name: Wake County Public Defender (NC IDS)
trackers.desc: Raleigh / Wake County public defender — part of North Carolina’s statewide Indigent Defense Services system, providing criminal defense to people who cannot afford counsel.

trackers.name: Ramsey County Public Defender (MN 2nd District)
trackers.desc: St. Paul / Ramsey County public defender (Minnesota’s Second Judicial District) — represents adults and juveniles accused of crimes and parents/children in child-protection cases, via the Minnesota Board of Public Defense.

trackers.name: Neighborhood Justice Center (St. Paul)
trackers.desc: St. Paul community law office — comprehensive criminal defense for low-income people with an emphasis on outreach to communities of color across the East Metro.

trackers.name: Fairfax Public Defender (Virginia IDC)
trackers.desc: Public defender serving the City and County of Fairfax — one of the Virginia Indigent Defense Commission’s offices, handling trial and appellate cases in General District, Juvenile and Circuit Courts.

trackers.name: Richmond Public Defender (Virginia IDC)
trackers.desc: Public defender for the City of Richmond — a Virginia Indigent Defense Commission office providing trial and appellate criminal defense.

trackers.name: Norfolk Public Defender (Virginia IDC)
trackers.desc: Public defender for the City of Norfolk — a Virginia Indigent Defense Commission office providing indigent criminal defense in the Tidewater region.

trackers.name: Montgomery County Public Defender (Dayton)
trackers.desc: Dayton / Montgomery County public defender — grew from the 1972 Miami Valley Public Defender Project; represents people charged with any level of felony or jailable misdemeanor, juveniles in delinquency/traffic/truancy cases, and extradition matters.

trackers.name: Oakland County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Oakland County, Michigan public defender (metro Detroit suburbs) — indigent criminal defense under the county’s Indigent Defense Services division.

trackers.name: Eaton County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Eaton County, Michigan public defender (Lansing area) — representation of indigent people charged with misdemeanors and felonies.

trackers.name: Oklahoma County Public Defender (OKC)
trackers.desc: Oklahoma City / Oklahoma County public defender — defense for indigent people in misdemeanors, felonies, capital crimes and appeals, plus court-appointed representation of children in juvenile/deprived cases and people in civil-commitment hearings.

trackers.name: Tulsa County Public Defender
trackers.desc: Tulsa County, Oklahoma public defender — indigent criminal defense for county residents.

trackers.name: Victoria Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid body for Victoria — duty lawyers at courts and tribunals across the state, a Legal Help phone line and live chat, with services in 34 languages.

trackers.name: Legal Aid NSW
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for New South Wales, providing criminal, family and civil representation to people who cannot afford a lawyer.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Queensland
trackers.desc: Provides legal assistance to financially disadvantaged Queenslanders, including criminal defence and duty lawyer services.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Western Australia
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for Western Australia.

trackers.name: Legal Services Commission of South Australia
trackers.desc: South Australia’s statutory legal aid provider, offering criminal defence and legal advice services.

trackers.name: Tasmania Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Statutory legal aid commission for Tasmania.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Northern Territory
trackers.desc: Northern Territory Legal Aid Commission — criminal, family and civil legal assistance.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Ontario
trackers.desc: Provides legal help in English and French to financially eligible low-income Ontarians — criminal duty counsel, certificates for private lawyers, and a Service Finder for local legal clinics and courthouses.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Alberta
trackers.desc: Provincial legal aid for Albertans who cannot afford a lawyer — criminal defence, family and protection matters, with a lawyer directory for applicants.

trackers.name: Legal Aid BC
trackers.desc: Free legal information, advice and representation for BC residents — 34 community offices, Legal Aid Navigators, Parents Legal Centres and First Nations/Indigenous Courts.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Saskatchewan
trackers.desc: Provides criminal and civil legal services to people financially unable to afford counsel, with offices across the province.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Manitoba
trackers.desc: Pays for or helps pay for a lawyer for adults and youth facing criminal charges, and for family and social-benefits matters.

trackers.name: Commission des services juridiques (Québec)
trackers.desc: The agency responsible for ensuring legal aid is provided to every eligible person in Quebec; applications are made at the local legal aid office.

trackers.name: Legal Aid New Brunswick (NBLASC)
trackers.desc: Provides legal assistance to low-income people in criminal and certain family matters; applications are completed at the legal aid office.

trackers.name: Nova Scotia Legal Aid
trackers.desc: Thirteen community-based law offices providing criminal, family and civil legal services; applications can be made online.

trackers.name: Newfoundland and Labrador Legal Aid Commission
trackers.desc: Ensures people with limited financial means have access to legal counsel across Newfoundland and Labrador.

trackers.name: Nunavut Legal Aid (Legal Services Board)
trackers.desc: Legal Services Board of Nunavut — criminal, family and poverty law services across the territory.

trackers.name: NWT Legal Aid Commission
trackers.desc: Oversees legal aid clinics in every administrative region of the Northwest Territories.

trackers.name: Yukon Legal Services Society
trackers.desc: Territorial legal aid for Yukon residents facing criminal charges and family matters.

trackers.name: Scottish Legal Aid Board (SLAB)
trackers.desc: Administers publicly funded legal assistance in Scotland under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 — criminal and civil legal aid, eligibility checks and a solicitor finder.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Agency (England & Wales)
trackers.desc: Executive agency of the Ministry of Justice administering the legal aid scheme in England and Wales, and funder of the Public Defender Service.

trackers.name: Legal Aid Agency — Wales
trackers.desc: Check eligibility for legal aid in England and Wales and connect to Civil Legal Advice; Welsh-language advisers available.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Gauteng
trackers.desc: Provincial office list for Legal Aid South Africa in Gauteng — local and satellite offices near the courts they serve, with heads of office and contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Western Cape
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Western Cape local and satellite offices, including court-based access points.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Eastern Cape
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Eastern Cape local and satellite offices with contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — KwaZulu-Natal
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s KwaZulu-Natal provincial, local and satellite offices — Durban, Pinetown, Empangeni and rural access points, with heads of office and contact details.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Limpopo
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Limpopo offices, including Louis Trichardt, Musina, Modimolle and Lephalale.

trackers.name: Legal Aid SA — Mpumalanga
trackers.desc: Legal Aid South Africa’s Mpumalanga offices — including one inside the Mpumalanga High Court building at Mbombela.

trackers.name: Delhi State Legal Services Authority (DSLSA)
trackers.desc: Constituted under the Legal Services Authorities Act 1987 — free legal services to marginalised people, legal aid for accused persons through the Legal Aid Defense Counsel System and district advocate panels, victim compensation, and Lok Adalats.

trackers.name: Maharashtra State Legal Services Authority (MSLSA)
trackers.desc: State legal services authority giving effect to Articles 14 and 39-A of the Indian Constitution — free legal aid and Lok Adalats under the object “Access to Justice for All”.

trackers.name: Tamil Nadu State Legal Services Authority (TNSLSA)
trackers.desc: Provides free legal aid under Article 39-A so that justice is not denied to any citizen by reason of poverty or disability.

trackers.name: Defensoria Pública do Estado de São Paulo (DPESP)
trackers.desc: Free and comprehensive legal assistance to people who cannot afford a lawyer — criminal defence, judicial and extrajudicial representation in state courts, with unit addresses and a free 0800 booking line.

trackers.name: Defensoria Pública do Estado do Rio de Janeiro (DPRJ)
trackers.desc: Public institution offering free, comprehensive legal assistance and guidance to citizens who cannot pay, and defending human rights and the rights of people in vulnerable situations.
